subtopics.title: Components
subtopics.content:
- **54 clan cards** in 6 colors, numbered 1-9 each
- **9 stone markers** (boundary stones)
- **10 tactics cards** (optional variant)

subtopics.title: Table Setup
subtopics.content:
1. **Place 9 stone markers** in a row between players.
2. **Shuffle clan cards** and deal 6 to each player.
3. **Place remaining cards** as draw pile.
4. **Tactics cards** (if used) form a separate draw pile.

subtopics.title: Player Setup
subtopics.content:
- **6 cards in hand.**
- **Each stone** has space for 3 cards per side.

subtopics.title: Game Overview
subtopics.content: Schotten Totten is a 2-player card game. Play cards on your side of 9 boundary stones to form the strongest 3-card formations. Claim stones by proving your formation beats your opponent's. Win 5 stones or 3 adjacent stones.

subtopics.title: Turn Structure
subtopics.content:
On your turn:
1. **Play 1 card** from your hand to your side of any unclaimed stone (max 3 cards per side).
2. **Claim stones** (optional) — If you can prove your formation wins at any stone.
3. **Draw 1 card** from the draw pile.

subtopics.title: Key Actions
subtopics.content:
#### Formation Rankings (Strongest to Weakest)
1. **Color Run** (Straight Flush) — 3 consecutive numbers, same color. Highest sum wins ties.
2. **Three of a Kind** — 3 cards of the same number. Higher number wins.
3. **Color** (Flush) — 3 cards of same color. Highest sum wins.
4. **Run** (Straight) — 3 consecutive numbers, different colors. Highest sum wins.
5. **Sum** (High Card) — Any 3 cards. Highest sum wins.

#### Claiming Stones
- A stone can be claimed when you can prove your formation is unbeatable.
- You may claim even before opponent plays 3 cards, if no remaining cards could beat you.
- Both players' played cards and the cards remaining in the deck are public information for proving claims.

subtopics.title: Special Rules
subtopics.content:
#### Tactics Variant (Battle Line)
- Shuffle 10 tactics cards as a separate deck.
- You may draw from either deck.
- Tactics cards have special effects (wild cards, card removal, etc.).
- Cannot play more tactics than your opponent + 1.

#### Proof of Claim
- When claiming, demonstrate that no combination of unplayed cards could form a stronger opposing formation.

subtopics.title: Scoring and Endgame
subtopics.content:
#### Win Conditions
- **5 stones** — Claim any 5 of the 9 stones.
- **3 adjacent** — Claim 3 stones in a row (breakthrough).

#### No points — pure win/loss.

subtopics.title: Beginner Tips
subtopics.content:
- **Color runs are king** — They're the strongest formation; build them when possible.
- **Spread your plays** — Don't commit all cards to one stone.
- **Claim early** — Prove claims as soon as possible to lock in stones.
- **Watch the draw pile** — Track which cards have been played to know what's possible.

subtopics.title: Key Strategies
subtopics.content:
- **Card counting** — With 54 cards and perfect information of plays, you can calculate exact odds.
- **Adjacent pressure** — Focus on claiming 3 adjacent stones for a quick win.
- **Sacrifice stones** — Concede hopeless stones to concentrate strength elsewhere.
- **Proof timing** — Claim stones early using logical proof even before opponent finishes their formation.
